description: A polished public-service-announcement graphic for ICE's VOICE program. The right two-thirds is a warm, soft-focus stock photograph of two pairs of hands clasped together in a comforting gesture, with a seated person (in a light blue shirt and gray pants) being consoled, evoking compassion and support. The left third is a blue overlay panel with white text reading 'If you're the victim of a crime linked to immigration, we're here to' followed by 'HELP' in large bold caps. An orange angled accent shape sits at the right edge. A white footer bar carries contact info in blue and orange: '1-855-48-VOICE' and 'ICE.GOV/VOICE', plus the VOICE logo (a stylized blue 'V') with text 'VOICE — VICTIMS OF IMMIGRATION CRIME ENGAGEMENT.' The messaging technique is a sympathetic, victim-centered appeal that frames ICE as a helping institution while reinforcing the politically charged 'immigration crime' framing that links crime to immigrants. Target audience is crime victims and the broader public; intent is outreach plus normalizing the VOICE office. This is a recurring template (also used in 3_1991960082607718400).
notable_text: If you're the victim of a crime linked to immigration, we're here to HELP; 1-855-48-VOICE; ICE.GOV/VOICE; VOICE — VICTIMS OF IMMIGRATION CRIME ENGAGEMENT
